messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
when did the last state became a state

content.message:
To determine when the last state became a state, I need to identify the most recent state that was admitted into the United States. This would involve finding information about the states and their admission dates.

Step 1: Search for information about U.S. states and their admission dates. I'll first explore the list of states and potentially look for the latest admission date.

I will search for properties related to the admission of states in the U.S. to determine when the last state became a state.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. fee (wd:P2555 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "admission"), also known as "admission", "charge", "entrance fee", "entrance fees", "entry fee", etc.:
  - fee or toll payable to use, transit or enter the subject ... do NOT use it for an ongoing fee, tuition fee or trading fee)
2. admission rate (wd:P5822 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "acceptance rate":
  - ratio of the number of applicants admitted to the total number of applicants
3. diplomatic mission sent (wd:P531 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "embassy"), also known as "embassy", "resident mission":
  - location of diplomatic mission, i.e. consulate of A in the capital city of B
4. country of citizenship (wd:P27 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "citizenship"), also known as "citizen of", "citizenship", "country", "(legal) nationality", "nation", etc.:
  - the object is a country that recognizes the subject as its citizen
5. exploitation mark number (wd:P2755 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "exploitation visa"), also known as "exploitation visa", "film distribution certificate":
  - number of the exploitation mark of a movie in France
6. connector (wd:P2935 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "ports"), also known as "electrical connector", "has connector", "port", "ports":
  - physical connectors which the device has/supports
7. admission yield rate (wd:P10263 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "acceptance rate", "yield", "yield rate":
  - percentage of admitted applicants who accept the admission offer
8. applies to jurisdiction (wd:P1001 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "applicable country"), also known as "applicable area", "applicable country", "applicable geographic place", "applicable jurisdiction", "applicable location", etc.:
  - the item (institution, law, public office, public register, ... jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
9. destination of transfer (wd:P12694 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "destination"), also known as "destination", "end point", "receiver", "recipient", "target", etc.:
  - entity that a transferred item comes to be associated with as a result of this process
10. country (wd:P17 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "host country", "land", "sovereign state", "state":
  - sovereign state that this item is in (not to be used for human beings)

content.message:
I need to refine my search. The property "admission to United States" did not yield relevant results. Instead, I should look for properties related to "state admission" or "admission date" of states to the United States.

Step 2: Search for properties related to the admission of states or the date of admission.

tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. fee (wd:P2555 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "admission"), also known as "admission", "charge", "entrance fee", "entrance fees", "entry fee", etc.:
  - fee or toll payable to use, transit or enter the subject ... do NOT use it for an ongoing fee, tuition fee or trading fee)
2. admission rate (wd:P5822 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "acceptance rate":
  - ratio of the number of applicants admitted to the total number of applicants
3. date of application (wd:P12413 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - date of a application/request that led to the process of a ... issuance, or obtaining of the position of an identifier
4. announcement date (wd:P6949 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "date announced"), also known as "announced date", "announced in", "announced on", "date", "date announced", etc.:
  - time of the first public presentation of a subject by the creator, of information by the media
5. date of promulgation (wd:P7589 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "adoption date"), also known as "adoption date", "assent", "date of adoption", "date of approval", "date of assent", etc.:
  - date on which a piece of legislation formally became law (whether by assent or some other procedure)
6. admission yield rate (wd:P10263 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "acceptance rate", "yield", "yield rate":
  - percentage of admitted applicants who accept the admission offer
7. commercialization date (wd:P5204 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "availability date"), also known as "availability date", "date of commercialization", "marketed", "product release date", "release date":
  - date when an invention was first commercialized
8. inception (wd:P571 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "commencement date"), also known as "built", "commenced on date", "commencement date", "completed", "composed", etc.:
  - time when an entity begins to exist; for date of official opening use P1619
9. end time (wd:P582 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "fall date"), also known as "abolished", "cease date", "cease operation", "cease time", "closed", etc.:
  - moment when an entity ceases to exist and a statement stops being entirely valid or no longer be true
10. UTC date of spacecraft landing (wd:P620 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "landing date"), also known as "date of spacecraft landing", "landing date", "landing time", "spacecraft landing date", "spacecraft landing time", etc.:
  - date of spacecraft landing in UTC

content.message:
The property "admission date" is not directly available. However, I notice that "date of promulgation" and "inception" are relevant, as "inception" refers to the time when an entity begins to exist, which could be when a state becomes a state.

Step 3: Search for properties related to the "state" entity to find properties related to when states were admitted to the United States.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. has part(s) (wd:P527 as wdt/p, matched via "includes"), also known as "amalgamation of", "assembled from", "assembled out of", "composed of", "comprised of", etc.:
  - part of this subject; inverse property of "part of" (P361). See also "has parts of the class" (P2670).
2. different from (wd:P1889 as wdt/p, matched via "not"), also known as "confused with", "different than", "different to", "differs from", "diff from", etc.:
  - item that is different from another item, with which it may be confused
3. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
4. contains the administrative territorial entity (wd:P150 as wdt/p, matched via "contains"), also known as "contains", "contains administrative territorial entity", "divided into", "divides into", "has administrative divisions", etc.:
  - (list of) direct subdivisions of an administrative territorial entity
5. topic's main Wikimedia portal (wd:P1151 as wdt/p, matched via "portal"), also known as "main portal", "portal":
  - Wikimedia portal associated with this topic
6. said to be the same as (wd:P460 as wdt/p, matched via "equal"), also known as "considered the same as", "considered to be the same as", "conspecific with", "could be", "could be equal to", etc.:
  - this item is said to be the same as that item, though this may be uncertain or disputed
7. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "is a"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
8. studied by (wd:P2579 as wdt/p, matched via "domain"), also known as "domain", "in branch of science", "is studied by", "is studied in", "researched by", etc.:
  - subject is studied by this science or domain
9. subclass of (wd:P279 as wdt/p, matched via "form of"), also known as "all members of this class also belong to", "category of", "form of", "has superclass", "is a class of", etc.:
  - this item is a subclass (subset) of that item; ALL instances ... is a subclass of mountain; Everest is an instance of mountain
10. opposite of (wd:P461 as wdt/p, matched via "opposite"), also known as "adversary", "antagonist", "contrasted to", "contrast of", "has opposite", etc.:
  - item that is in some way the opposite of this item

I see that the property "contains the administrative territorial entity" (P150) is relevant — it indicates that the United States contains its states. However, I still need to find the date when the last state became a state.

Step 4: Search for the date of admission or inception of states in the United States.
I will search for properties related to the state's admission or inception date.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. part of (wd:P361 as wdt/p, matched via "of"), also known as "assembly of", "branch of", "chain", "collateral branch of", "component of", etc.:
  - object of which the subject is a part (if this subject is ... "has part" (P527, see also "has parts of the class" (P2670))
2. coordinate location (wd:P625 as wdt/p, matched via "position"), also known as "co-ordinate location", "co-ordinates", "coordinates", "co-ords", "coords", etc.:
  - geocoordinates of the subject. For Earth, please note that only the WGS84 geodetic datum is currently supported
3. located in the administrative territorial entity (wd:P131 as wdt/p, matched via "in"), also known as "administrative territorial entity", "administrative territory", "city", "happens in", "in", etc.:
  - the item is located on the territory of the following ... the item falls only partially into the administrative entity
4. has part(s) (wd:P527 as wdt/p, matched via "contain"), also known as "amalgamation of", "assembled from", "assembled out of", "composed of", "comprised of", etc.:
  - part of this subject; inverse property of "part of" (P361). See also "has parts of the class" (P2670).
5. heritage designation (wd:P1435 as wdt/p, matched via "listed status"), also known as "designation", "heritage status", "legal protection", "listed status", "listing", etc.:
  - heritage designation of a cultural or natural site
6. Library of Congress authority ID (wd:P244 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "P244"), also known as "Authority LCCN", "LCAuth ID", "LCAuth identifier", "LCCN", "LC Control Number", etc.:
  - Library of Congress name authority (persons, families, ... by 8-10 digits (see regex). For manifestations, use P1144]
7. located on street (wd:P669 as wdt/p, matched via "is on"), also known as "address street", "is on", "located at street (item)", "on street", "road", etc.:
  - street, road, square, or trail, where the item is located. To ... P6375 "street address", if there is no item for the street
8. street address (wd:P6375 as wdt/p, matched via "address"), also known as "address", "full address", "located at street address", "mail address", "mailing address", etc.:
  - full street address where subject is located. Include ... use also P669 if the street has its own separate item
9. Google Arts & Culture partner ID (wd:P4702 as wdt/p, matched via "P4702"):
  - identifier for a museum or other partner on the Google Arts & Culture website
10. VIAF cluster ID (wd:P214 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "P214"), also known as "VIAF", "VIAF ID", "VIAF identifier", "viaf.org ID", "Virtual International Authority File":
  - identifier for the Virtual International Authority File ... note: VIAF is a cluster, the ID can include multiple items

  - street, road, square, or trail, where the item is located. To ... P6375 "street address", if there is no item for the street
5. topic's main category (wd:P910 as wdt/p, matched via "category"), also known as "article's main category", "category", "category for this topic", "catmain", "has category", etc.:
  - main Wikimedia category
6. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "is a"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
7. image (wd:P18 as wdt/p, matched via "illustration"), also known as "album artwork", "album cover", "artwork", "camera picture", "Commons image", etc.:
  - image of relevant illustration of the subject; if available, ... map, flag image, signature image, logo image, collage image)
8. official website (wd:P856 as wdt/p, matched via "homepage (official)"), also known as "home page", "homepage", "home page (official)", "homepage (official)", "official homepage", etc.:
  - URL of the official page of an item (current or former). ... see the "Hijacked or dead websites" section of the Talk page
9. nighttime view (wd:P3451 as wdt/p, matched via "night image"), also known as "image at night", "image of the subject at night, or at least in twilight", "image of the subject at night or in twilight", "night image", "night photo", etc.:
  - image at night or in twilight
10. coordinate location (wd:P625 as wdt/p, matched via "position"), also known as "co-ordinate location", "co-ordinates", "coordinates", "co-ords", "coords", etc.:
  - geocoordinates of the subject. For Earth, please note that only the WGS84 geodetic datum is currently supported

question: when did the last state became a state
